situation: audit-2026-07-06-022 finding M-010: pipeline.py:64 _load() parses JSON and returns dict without any field validation. Downstream code (cmd_next, cmd_mark, cmd_status) assumes state['slug'], state['date'], state['steps'] (list of dicts with id/status/phase), state['mode'] exist. Corrupt or tampered state files cause silent KeyError or wrong phase routing. Companion fix: _save() non-atomic write risks torn state on crash.
action: Add _validate_state() that checks required fields (slug/date/mode as str, steps as list of dicts each with id/status/phase str). Call it in _load() before returning. Refactor _save() to atomic tempfile + os.replace pattern mirroring M-009 fix. Fail fast with clear SystemExit message directing operator to re-init.

audit_findings.finding: _load() json.loads result returned without schema validation; corrupt state causes downstream KeyError/silent misrouting
audit_findings.remediation: Add _validate_state() gating required fields (slug/date/mode str, steps list of {id,status,phase} dicts); fail fast with SystemExit directing re-init. Also atomic-write _save via tempfile+os.replace.
